aggressive_mode_contract.cost_policy: use local scripts, cached reports, static dashboards, and free or low-cost model routes first
aggressive_mode_contract.github_policy: manual or path-gated actions only unless owner approves more GitHub minutes
hard_limits: no production deployment without explicit owner approval
what_it_runs: scan every bot registry and connection report
queue every bot for sandbox-first test or rebuild packets
run local syntax and smoke checks before GitHub Actions
run GitHub cost saver and repository stewardship scans
refresh AI model routing and Gemini low-cost paths
summarize approval gates and next best actions

always_on_strategy.plain_english: Buddy keeps the system moving by rotating cheap local/report-based cycles and queued work packets. Bots do not need expensive always-on cloud containers to stay useful.
runtime_layers: Cloud Run request-based service that sleeps when idle
free or low-cost AI model routes
always_on_strategy.success_condition: Every bot has a current packet, status, test path, resource path, and approval boundary.

can_do_with_repo_access: route prompts to the best available AI resource
write safe operation packets for every bot
buddy_codex_parity_goal.positioning: Buddy should behave like a governed coding and operations teammate inside DreamCo.
requires_owner_approval: change GitHub billing or paid products

codex_style_capabilities.approval: No approval for read-only local scans.
codex_style_capabilities.buddy_can: Scan files, reports, bot profiles, configs, tests, workflows, and dashboards.
codex_style_capabilities.cheap_path: Use local file reads and generated JSON reports before network calls.
codex_style_capabilities.label: Repository reader

codex_style_capabilities.approval: Owner approval before push, merge, deploy, or destructive edits.
codex_style_capabilities.buddy_can: Draft scoped code, config, test, and documentation changes for review.
codex_style_capabilities.cheap_path: Use deterministic templates and smallest-capable AI routes before premium models.
codex_style_capabilities.label: Code editor

codex_style_capabilities.approval: Owner approval before increasing scheduled CI frequency or paid runner usage.
codex_style_capabilities.buddy_can: Run syntax checks, unit tests, smoke tests, report freshness checks, and failure triage.
codex_style_capabilities.cheap_path: Run locally first; use GitHub Actions only for release evidence or remote-only checks.
codex_style_capabilities.label: Test and debug runner

codex_style_capabilities.approval: Owner approval before closing, merging, rebasing, or force-pushing PR branches.
codex_style_capabilities.buddy_can: Prepare PR summaries, compare goals to repository proof, and queue fixes for failed checks.
codex_style_capabilities.cheap_path: Use cached GitHub triage reports and local diffs before API refreshes.
codex_style_capabilities.label: Pull request helper

codex_style_capabilities.approval: Owner approval before paid always-on loops, premium batches, or production customer data.
codex_style_capabilities.buddy_can: Pick local/static/free/cheap/premium model routes based on task risk and quality needs.
codex_style_capabilities.cheap_path: Prefer local reports, cache, Gemini low-cost routes, and small models for drafts.
codex_style_capabilities.label: AI model router

codex_style_capabilities.approval: Owner approval before customer outreach, money movement, deploys, or account changes.
codex_style_capabilities.buddy_can: Create supervised work packets for every bot so the fleet keeps learning, testing, and packaging.
codex_style_capabilities.cheap_path: Queue work packets and summaries instead of running every bot as a paid live service.
codex_style_capabilities.label: Bot orchestrator

ai_cost_guardrails: Use local deterministic reports before model calls.
Use cached outputs before repeated model calls.
Route drafts, summaries, classification, and sandbox planning through free or low-cost models first.
Escalate to premium models only for failed cheap-model evals, high-impact client work, or approved production tasks.
Block always-on paid model loops without a budget cap and owner approval.
github_cost_guardrails: Prefer GitHub Pages, static reports, and local scripts before recurring Actions jobs.
Keep scheduled workflows manual, path-gated, or low-frequency until revenue justifies more runs.
Set artifact retention to 1-7 days for diagnostics and only keep longer release evidence intentionally.
Avoid paid Codespaces and hosted runners when the same check can run locally on the owner laptop.
Never bypass billing, quotas, permissions, or platform terms.
cost_control_policy.goal: Run useful 24-hour learning loops without surprise bills.

github_free_cheap_plan.artifact_policy: 1-7 day retention for diagnostics; longer only for release evidence.
github_free_cheap_plan.default_hosting: GitHub Pages for static dashboards and docs when possible.
github_free_cheap_plan.runner_policy: Local laptop first; GitHub-hosted only when needed for proof; paid larger/macOS runners avoided unless approved.

mission: Make Buddy a governed Codex-style coding, debugging, orchestration, and operations teammate while keeping DreamCo free or cheap by default.
next_actions: Keep Cloud Run min instances at 0 and max instances at 1 until revenue supports scale.
Move expensive scheduled workflows to manual or path-gated triggers.
Use Buddy operation packets for 24-hour progress instead of paid always-on bot containers.
Add budget alerts before enabling recurring paid AI, GitHub, or cloud workloads.
